"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwMjU5NTc3OTU5MDQ4NTE5Njg=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "* $LPTH A bit of a glaze post below but I wanted to write something up because of the pictured quote inspired me * When I first invested in $LPTH over a year ago, my initial thesis was largely driven by geopolitical tailwinds, especially China’s germanium export restrictions and the implications for domestic supply chains. But the reason I’ve continued to hold and add more shares very quickly evolved. And one aspect that truly strengthened my conviction in my investment is the leadership of @skrubin. Under his direction, the company has very clearly positioned itself at the forefront of EO/IR systems, threat detection, and advanced optics... markets with durable, long-term demand driven by national security and technological acceleration. And this current company positioning is not solely a case of “preparation meeting opportunity” with BlackDiamond serving as an alternative to germanium at the right moment. While that was an important inflection point no doubt, what gives me greater confidence going forward now is how Sam is actively compounding that advantage. He’s iterating on the core technology with next-generation BDNL materials, expanding applications into air defense and space-based systems, strengthening relationships with prime contractors like $LMT, $LHX, $ESLT and Anduril, pursuing strategic M&A (G5 and AMI), and methodically moving the company further up the value chain. These distinctions truly matter as some leaders benefit from favorable macro conditions while others build enduring platforms on top of them. Sam is clearly doing the latter. He’s turning a geopolitical tailwind into a structural competitive advantage and that’s what sets up LightPath not just to participate in this current cycle, but to compound through it. I truly think that the quote “The secret to investing is to invest in unstoppable people building inevitable things” genuinely applies to @skrubin and it clearly comes across with every investor engagement he participates in and not because of any sort of hype, but because of his clarity, his strategic intent, and consistent execution. He’s one of the few CEOs whose every single interview, earnings call, and even social media update I genuinely look forward to as an investor. Leaders like Sam are how companies transform and flourish, and I truly believe that over the next 5–10 years, LightPath has the potential to become the next FLIR Systems (now part Teledyne) or Leonardo DRS."}}}, "rest_id": "2025957796156510217", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}}}, "rest_id": "2057884513276240309", "unmention_data": {}}

It has now been two months since I published my first deep dive on $SIVE 

The stock is up 10x since then and, honestly, I think it is now massively overextended.

SEK 21.7B market cap on SEK 306M of revenue.
More than 70x sales.

I remain bullish on the long-term photonics / AI connectivity thesis, and I was probably one of the first public bulls talking about the company. But even then, the current valuation is absolutely astronomical.

At this point, the market is already pricing in a brutal revenue ramp, massive conversion from NRE into product revenue, and near-perfect execution over the coming years.

That said, I’m not fucking selling

text:
$COHR, $MTSI, $STM and Hamamatsu quietly dominating SpaceX’s lasercomm supply chain is another real-world proof point that photonics demand is expanding beyond terrestrial data centers.

the post maps the space-qualified chain perfectly; precision optics/coatings/EDFAs, high-speed drivers/TIAs, PAT control ASICs, and high-sensitivity detectors. 

flight heritage is the ultimate moat in this market, and these four are already winning the first generation.

this is bullish for the broader photonics layer. 

hyperscalers need reliable low-latency global connectivity for AI data centers, edge computing, and the full physical stack. starlink-type orbital networks solve real bottlenecks that terrestrial grids can’t fix fast enough. the same laser and optical engine technology that powers inter-satellite links has direct read-through to terrestrial CPO and pluggable ramps.

this cycle rewards the execution plays solving proven physical bottlenecks that hyperscalers literally can’t pause for. photonics (lasers, optics, detectors) is one of those constraints, and the space demand layer just adds another structural tailwind on top of the terrestrial AI buildout.

the setup has legs. this one ages well.
